message:
fix(user.sh): recognize all SSH key formats when checking for existing keys

The existing key check in prompt_ssh_key() only matched keys starting with
"ssh-" (ssh-ed25519, ssh-rsa, ssh-dss). This missed valid OpenSSH key formats:

- ecdsa-sha2-nistp256, ecdsa-sha2-nistp384, ecdsa-sha2-nistp521
- sk-ssh-ed25519@openssh.com (security keys)
- sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com (security keys)

Users with ECDSA or hardware security keys in authorized_keys would be
incorrectly prompted to add a new key, even though one already exists.

The fix uses an extended grep pattern that matches all three prefix families:
- ssh-* (traditional RSA, Ed25519, DSA)
- ecdsa-sha2-* (ECDSA curves)
- sk-* (OpenSSH security key types)

This now matches the same formats accepted by the key validation regex
on line 244 of the same file.

files.patch:
@@ -194,8 +194,9 @@ prompt_ssh_key() {
     local authorized_keys="/root/.ssh/authorized_keys"
 
     # 1. Check if we already have a valid key
+    # Match all OpenSSH key formats: ssh-*, ecdsa-sha2-*, sk-* (security keys)
     if [[ -f "$authorized_keys" ]]; then
-        if grep -q "^ssh-" "$authorized_keys" 2>/dev/null; then
+        if grep -qE "^(ssh-|ecdsa-sha2-|sk-)" "$authorized_keys" 2>/dev/null; then
             log_detail "SSH key already present, skipping prompt"
             return 0
         fi
